inline_comments.message: How about checking for existence of WORKSPACE variable? Doing rm -rf on unchanged path seems quite dangerous...

This and other cloning has inherent version control issues. As we dicussed before, this seems to be the "normal" way now anyway so probably it is too hard to change it to something better now. But I would really like to see this documented somewhere that a new user is likely to read as the results can be very unexpected and confusing.

This and other DCAE TOSCA files are actually generated by Toscalab from TCA spec file. It is ok not to include this actual generation part right now, but at least refer to this source material in some good place in documentation. Later the actual generation should be included as well.
I guess these kinds definitions (user accounts) are destined to be used from some with the SDC core definitions? As DCAE tests use also the core, I don't think they could reasonably be different

Destroying everything under /data/ doesn't sound particularly dangerous to me - it's not a system dir or anything like that. If this actually is seen as a potential real problem though, attention should be paid also to the creation of the data dir in setup scripts which apply the same WORKSPACE variable across multiple CSIT setups (which should be a separate task and commit).

I couldn't agree more. In my opinion these checkouts should use the branch of the csit repo from which the test is executed - however, this is again something that should be done as a separate task (or even user story) and series of commits over the entire CSIT space as none of the existing suites do this quite right. For now checking the default master is fine for DCAE-DS CSIT since the test doesn't exist in any other branch.

The users originally come from testsuite/robot/resources/asdc_interface.robot, and there's supposed to be a lot of future refactoring of these in El Alto so I'm having big hopes regarding that effort.
